title: The socialmixr package
text: Contact matrices computed small pipeline composable functions: survey[expr] – filter participants contacts. assign_age_groups() – impute missing ages assign age groups. weigh() – optionally apply participant weights. compute_matrix() – compute mean contact matrix. symmetrise(), split_matrix(), per_capita() – optional post-processing. step separate function. POLYMOD data included package can loaded using example use generates contact matrix UK part POLYMOD study, age groups 0-1, 1-5, 5-15 15+ years. contains mean number contacts member age group (row) reported members another age group (column). assign_age_groups() prepares survey matrix computation. imputes participant contact ages available ranges, drops keeps rows missing ages (configurable via missing_participant_age missing_contact_age), adds age.group contact.age.group columns using age_limits supply: resulting survey object can inspected, subset, passed number weigh() calls compute_matrix().
code: data(polymod) polymod[country == "United Kingdom"] |>   assign_age_groups(age_limits = c(0, 1, 5, 15)) |>   compute_matrix() #>  #> ── Contact matrix (4 age groups) ── #>  #> Ages: "[0,1)", "[1,5)", "[5,15)", and "[15,Inf)" #> Participants: 1011 #>  #>           contact.age.group #> age.group       [0,1)     [1,5)   [5,15) [15,Inf) #>   [0,1)    0.40000000 0.8000000 1.266667 5.933333 #>   [1,5)    0.11250000 1.9375000 1.462500 5.450000 #>   [5,15)   0.02450980 0.5049020 7.946078 6.215686 #>   [15,Inf) 0.03230337 0.3581461 1.290730 9.594101 uk_grouped <- polymod[country == "United Kingdom"] |>   assign_age_groups(age_limits = c(0, 1, 5, 15))  head(uk_grouped$participants[, c("part_id", "part_age", "age.group")]) #>    part_id part_age age.group #>      <int>    <int>    <fctr> #> 1:    4536        0     [0,1) #> 2:    4538        0     [0,1) #> 3:    4540        0     [0,1) #> 4:    4541        0     [0,1) #> 5:    4542        0     [0,1) #> 6:    4546        0     [0,1) head(uk_grouped$contacts[, c("part_id", "cnt_age", "contact.age.group")]) #>    part_id cnt_age contact.age.group #>      <int>   <int>            <fctr> #> 1:    4517       4             [1,5) #> 2:    4517      40          [15,Inf) #> 3:    4517      31          [15,Inf) #> 4:    4517      52          [15,Inf) #> 5:    4517      29          [15,Inf) #> 6:    4517      59          [15,Inf)

title: The socialmixr package
text: get idea uncertainty contact matrices, participants can resampled replacement. short helper replicates participant (matching contact) rows occurrence resampled ID, duplicates preserved:
code: bootstrap <- function(survey) {   sampled_ids <- sample(     unique(survey$participants$part_id),     replace = TRUE   )   survey$participants <- survey$participants[     list(sampled_ids), on = "part_id"   ]   survey$contacts <- survey$contacts[     list(sampled_ids),     on = "part_id",     nomatch = NULL,     allow.cartesian = TRUE   ]   survey }  uk <- polymod[country == "United Kingdom"] |>   assign_age_groups(age_limits = c(0, 1, 5, 15))  m <- suppressWarnings(   replicate(n = 5, uk |> bootstrap() |> compute_matrix()) ) mr <- Reduce("+", lapply(m["matrix", ], function(x) x / ncol(m))) mr #>           contact.age.group #> age.group       [0,1)     [1,5)    [5,15) [15,Inf) #>   [0,1)    0.29811966 1.8413675  2.519658 10.67547 #>   [1,5)    0.19834154 4.2123592  3.341740 10.10003 #>   [5,15)   0.04590587 0.9077375 16.780686 12.80115 #>   [15,Inf) 0.06085092 0.7662912  2.859090 18.85493

title: The socialmixr package
text: Obtaining symmetric contact matrices, splitting components (see ) population-based participant weights require information underlying demographic composition survey population. represented data.frame columns lower.age.limit (lower end age group) population (number people age group). can construct comparable data frame pass relevant post-processing step: recent UN World Population Prospects data, wpp2024 package available GitHub (remotes::install_github("PPgp/wpp2024")): survey_country_population() looks country-specific population data wpp2017 package, optional (Suggests) dependency socialmixr; install separately use function. Passing population data directly () preferred possible:
code: custom_pop <- data.frame(   lower.age.limit = c(0, 18, 60),   population = c(12000000, 35000000, 20000000) ) polymod[country == "United Kingdom"] |>   assign_age_groups(age_limits = c(0, 18, 60)) |>   compute_matrix() |>   symmetrise(survey_pop = custom_pop) #>           contact.age.group #> age.group    [0,18)  [18,60) [60,Inf) #>   [0,18)   7.813187 5.899817 1.166637 #>   [18,60)  2.022794 7.931429 2.084481 #>   [60,Inf) 0.699982 3.647842 1.918033 data("popAge1dt", package = "wpp2024") uk_pop <- popAge1dt[name == "United Kingdom" & year == 2020,   .(lower.age.limit = age, population = pop * 1000) ] polymod[country == "United Kingdom"] |>   assign_age_groups(age_limits = c(0, 18, 60)) |>   compute_matrix() |>   symmetrise(survey_pop = uk_pop) #>           contact.age.group #> age.group     [0,18)  [18,60)  [60,Inf) #>   [0,18)   7.8131868 5.490727 0.8787841 #>   [18,60)  2.1528265 7.931429 1.7516352 #>   [60,Inf) 0.7665599 3.896982 1.9180328 uk_pop_default <- survey_country_population(   polymod, countries = "United Kingdom" ) head(uk_pop_default) #>    lower.age.limit population #>              <int>      <num> #> 1:               0    3453670 #> 2:               5    3558887 #> 3:              10    3826567 #> 4:              15    3960166 #> 5:              20    3906577 #> 6:              25    3755132

title: Introduction to socialmixr
text: socialmixr provides small set composable functions perform one step turning survey contact matrix: survey[expr] – filter participants contacts expression. assign_age_groups() – impute missing ages assign participants contacts age groups. weigh() – optionally attach participant weights (day week, age, user-defined). compute_matrix() – compute mean contact matrix. symmetrise(), split_matrix(), per_capita() – optional post-processing. minimal example extracting matrix UK part POLYMOD: produces contact matrix age groups 0-1, 1-5, 5-15 15+ years. contains mean number contacts member age group (row) reported members another age group (column).

code: bootstrap <- function(survey) {   sampled_ids <- sample(     unique(survey$participants$part_id),     replace = TRUE   )   survey$participants <- survey$participants[     list(sampled_ids), on = "part_id"   ]   survey$contacts <- survey$contacts[     list(sampled_ids),     on = "part_id",     nomatch = NULL,     allow.cartesian = TRUE   ]   survey }  uk <- polymod[country == "United Kingdom"] |>   assign_age_groups(age_limits = c(0, 1, 5, 15))  m <- suppressWarnings(   replicate(n = 5, uk |> bootstrap() |> compute_matrix()) ) mr <- Reduce("+", lapply(m["matrix", ], function(x) x / ncol(m))) mr #>           contact.age.group #> age.group       [0,1)     [1,5)    [5,15) [15,Inf) #>   [0,1)    0.86707602 1.2341520  1.873860 10.49181 #>   [1,5)    0.25116125 3.6745839  2.798376 10.05348 #>   [5,15)   0.05037865 0.9228788 16.525251 12.29984 #>   [15,Inf) 0.06248427 0.6102873  2.696695 18.29206

title: Introduction to socialmixr
text: Participant weights commonly used align sample population characteristics terms temporal aspects age distribution. example, day week reported driving factor social contact behaviour, hence obtain weekly average, survey data represent weekly 2/5 distribution weekend/week days. align survey data distribution, one can obtain participant weights form : wday..week=5/7Nweekday/N 2/7Nweekend/Nw_{\textrm{day..week}} = \tfrac{5/7}{N_{\textrm{weekday}}/N} \text{    } \tfrac{2/7}{N_{\textrm{weekend}}/N} sample size NN, NweekdayN_{weekday} NweekendN_{weekend} number participants surveyed weekdays weekend days, respectively. Another driver social contact patterns age. improve representativeness survey data, age-specific weights can calculated : wage=Pa/PNa/Nw_{age} = \tfrac{P_{}\ /\ P}{N_{}\ /\ N} PP population size, PaP_a population fraction age aa, NN survey sample size NaN_a survey fraction age aa. combination age-specific temporal weights participant ii age aa can constructed : wi=wage*wday..weekw_{} = w_{\textrm{age}} * w_{\textrm{day..week}} social contact analysis based stratification splitting population non-overlapping groups, requires weights standardised weighted totals within mutually exclusive cells equal known population totals (Kolenikov 2016). post-stratification cells need mutually exclusive cover whole population. compute_matrix() applies post-stratification normalisation within age groups. weigh() composable: call multiplies new weights participants’ weight column. POLYMOD, dayofweek column uses 0 Sunday 6 Saturday, weekdays 1-5 weekend days 0 6: first weigh() call assigns weekday participants total weight 5 weekend participants total weight 2 (weekly 5/2 split). second call post-stratifies population structure uk_pop (passed data frame).
code: polymod[country == "United Kingdom"] |>   assign_age_groups(age_limits = c(0, 18, 60)) |>   weigh("dayofweek", target = c(5, 2), groups = list(1:5, c(0, 6))) |>   weigh("part_age", target = uk_pop) |>   compute_matrix() #>           contact.age.group #> age.group    [0,18)  [18,60)  [60,Inf) #>   [0,18)   7.637824 5.372202 0.4878625 #>   [18,60)  2.279212 7.924999 1.0941688 #>   [60,Inf) 1.187088 5.303965 2.2302108

title: Generate a contact matrix from diary survey data — contact_matrix
text: survey survey() object. countries limit one countries; NULL (default), use countries survey; can given country names 2-letter (ISO Alpha-2) country codes. survey_pop survey population – either data frame columns 'lower.age.limit' 'population', character vector giving name(s) country countries list can obtained via wpp_countries; NULL (default), use country populations chosen countries, countries survey countries NULL. age_limits lower limits age groups construct matrix. NULL (default), age limits inferred participant contact ages. filter filters apply data, given list form (column=filter_value) - contacts 'filter_value' 'column' considered. multiple filters given, applied independently sequence given. Default value NULL; filtering performed. counts whether return counts (instead means). symmetric whether make matrix symmetric, \(c_{ij}N_i = c_{ji}N_j\). split whether split contact matrix mean number contacts, age group (split product mean number contacts across whole population (mean.contacts), normalisation constant (normalisation) age-specific variation contacts (contacts)), multiplied assortativity matrix (assortativity) population multiplier (demography). detail , see "Getting Started" vignette. sample_participants whether sample participants randomly (replacement); done multiple times can used assess uncertainty generated contact matrices. See "Bootstrapping" section vignette . estimated_participant_age set "mean" (default), people whose ages given range (columns named "..._est_min" "..._est_max") exactly (column named "..._exact") age set mid-point range; set "sample", age sampled range; set "missing", age ranges treated missing estimated_contact_age set "mean" (default), contacts whose ages given range (columns named "..._est_min" "..._est_max") exactly (column named "..._exact") age set mid-point range; set "sample", age sampled range; set "missing", age ranges treated missing. missing_participant_age set "remove" (default), participants without age information removed; set "keep", participants missing age kept appear contact matrix row labelled "NA". missing_contact_age set "remove" (default), participants contacts without age information removed; set "sample", contacts without age information sampled contacts participants age group; set "keep", contacts missing age kept appear contact matrix column labelled "NA"; set "ignore", contacts without age information removed analysis (participants made kept). weights column name(s) participant data survey() object user-specified weights (default = empty vector). weigh_dayofweek whether weigh social contacts data day week (weight (5/7 / N_week / N) weekdays (2/7 / N_weekend / N) weekends). weigh_age whether weigh social contacts data age participants (vs. populations' age distribution). weight_threshold threshold value standardized weights running additional standardisation (default 'NA' = cutoff). symmetric_norm_threshold threshold value normalization weights symmetric = TRUE showing warning large differences size sub-populations likely result artefacts making matrix symmetric (default 2). sample_all_age_groups sampling participants (sample_participants = TRUE) fails sample participants one age groups; FALSE (default), corresponding rows set NA, TRUE sample discarded new one taken instead. sample_participants_max_tries maximum number attempts sample_all_age_groups = TRUE; defaults 1000. return_part_weights boolean return participant weights. return_demography boolean explicitly return demography data corresponds survey data (default 'NA' = demography data requested function parameters). per_capita whether return matrix contact rates per capita (default FALSE possible 'counts=TRUE' 'split=TRUE'). ... arguments pass get_survey(), check() pop_age() (especially column names). survey.pop, age.limits, sample.participants, estimated.participant.age, estimated.contact.age, missing.participant.age, missing.contact.age, weigh.dayofweek, weigh.age, weight.threshold, symmetric.norm.threshold, sample..age.groups, sample.participants.max.tries, return.part.weights, return.demography, per.capita Use underscore-separated versions arguments instead.

title: Draws an image plot of a contact matrix with a legend strip and the numeric values in the cells. — matrix_plot
text: mij contact matrix containing contact rates participants age (rows) contacts age j (columns). default matrix format contact_matrix(). min.legend color scale minimum (default = 0). Set NA use minimum value mij. max.legend color scale maximum (default = NA). Set NA use maximum value mij. num.digits number digits rounding contact rates (default = 2). Use NA disable . num.colors number color breaks (default = 50) main figure title xlab title x axis (default: "Age group (year)") ylab title y axis (default: "Contact age group (year)") legend.width width legend strip characters. Default 1. legend.mar width characters legend margin. Default 5.1. legend.shrink amount shrink size legend relative full height width plot. Default 0.9. cex.lab size x y labels (default: 1.2) cex.axis size axis labels (default: 0.8) cex.text size numeric values matrix (default: 1) color.palette color palette use (default: heat.colors()). examples topo.colors(), terrain.colors() hcl.colors(). User-defined functions also possible take number colors palette function argument.

title: socialmixr 0.6.0
text: release adds pipeline composable functions building contact matrices ([, assign_age_groups(), weigh(), compute_matrix(), symmetrise(), split_matrix(), per_capita()) contact_matrix S3 class. vignette README rewritten around pipeline (#288).

title: socialmixr 0.6.0
text: Minimum R version bumped 4.1.0 (3.5.0). Examples pipeline functions use native |> pipe, introduced 4.1.0. Terminal age group labels now use [N,Inf) notation instead N+ bracket notation used (e.g. [0,5), [5,15), [15,Inf)). matches contactmatrix package gives parseable interval notation across age groups. affects matrix dimnames age.group column $participants; code matches strings like "15+" need updating "[15,Inf)". Dash notation (e.g. "15+") unchanged.

title: socialmixr 0.6.0
text: New [.contact_survey method allows filtering survey objects expressions, e.g. polymod[country == "United Kingdom"] (#161). New weigh() function composable participant weighting: supports day--week groups, named target vectors, direct numeric columns, population post-stratification (#161). New compute_matrix() function computes contact matrix prepared survey. final step pipeline assign_age_groups() (optionally) weigh() (#161). New post-processing functions symmetrise(), split_matrix(), per_capita() operate compute_matrix() output. symmetrise() enforces reciprocity, split_matrix() decomposes mean contacts, normalisation, assortativity matrix, per_capita() converts per-capita rates. Example workflow (#161): Pipeline functions (compute_matrix(), symmetrise(), split_matrix(), per_capita()) return contact_matrix S3 class print(), plot(), .matrix() methods. class inherits list, existing code using $matrix $participants continues work. New contact_age_distribution() function extracts empirical age distribution contacts survey. Pass assign_age_groups(estimated_contact_age = ...) impute ages ranges sampling reference distribution instead uniformly. matters surveys many contacts broad age bands, since uniform sampling flatten age-assortativity. New agegroups_to_limits() function converts age group labels back lower age limits, inverse limits_to_agegroups(). compute_matrix() gains weight_threshold parameter cap extreme weights normalisation, matching contact_matrix() option (#131).
code: uk_pop <- data.frame(   lower.age.limit = c(0, 5, 15),   population = c(3500000, 6000000, 50000000) ) polymod[country == "United Kingdom"] |>   assign_age_groups(age_limits = c(0, 5, 15)) |>   compute_matrix() |>   symmetrise(survey_pop = uk_pop)

Enabled cyclocomp_linter, line_length_linter, object_usage_linter. Disabled indentation_linter (air handles indentation).
